children.title: 1. Is the applicant organization a domestic public or private nonprofit entity, including faith-based, and community-based organizations, Tribes, and tribal organizations?

children.title: 2. Does the applicant propose to perform a substantive role in the project (i.e., does not apply on behalf of another organization)?
children.content:
Suggested Resource(s): SF-424A[ | Project Narrative[ | Budget Narrative[] | Attachment 2: Bylaws | Attachment 3: Project Organizational Charte? | Form 5A: Services Provided " | Form 8: Health Center Agreements["
[ X ] Yes [_ ] No
3. Does the applicant propose to operate a health center that makes all required primary health care services available and accessible in the service area, either directly or through established arrangements, without regard for ability to pay? (Note that applicants may not propose to provide ONLY a single service or any subset of the required primary health care services.)
Suggested Resource(s): Project Narrative [ [ X ] Yes [ _ ] No

children.title: 4. Does the applicant propose to make General Primary Medical Care available directly (Column I) and/or through formal written contractual agreements in which the health center pays for the service (Column II)?
children.content:
Suggested Resource(s): Form 5A: Services Provided [ [ X ] Yes [_ ] No
5. Does the applicant propose to provide access to services for all individuals in the service area and target population? If a sub-population is targeted (e.g., a school-based site that targets school-aged children), will health center services be available and accessible to others seeking services at the proposed site(s)? Suggested Resource(s): Project Narrative [ [ X ] Yes [_] No

formFields.value: Form 5A: Services Provided [ [ X ] Yes [_ ] No

a new budget will need to be requested.
12. New or competing supplement applicant: Is at least one new full-time (operational 40 hours or more per week) permanent, fixed building site with a verifiable street address proposed on Form 5B? *If funding is only requested for MHC, the applicant may propose a new full-time seasonal (rather than permanent) service delivery site.
Suggested Resource(s): Form 5B: Service Sites[? | SF-424A["
[_] Yes [ _] No [ X ] N/A

formFields.value: Is at least one new full-time (operational 40 hours or more per week) permanent, fixed building site with a verifiable street address proposed on Form 5B? *If funding is only requested for MHC, the applicant may propose a new full-time seasonal (rather than permanent) service delivery site.

children.title: 13. New or competing supplement applicant: For Form 5B: Service Sites, does the applicant include:
children.content:
· Service area zip codes where the zip code patient percentages on the SAAT total at least 75 percent; or
. All service area zip codes if the sum of all zip code patient percentages on the SAAT is less than 75 percent?
Suggested Resource(s): Form 5B: Service Sites[? | SAATE"
[_] Yes [ _ ] No [ X ] N/A
14. New or competing supplement applicant requesting PHPC funding: Does the applicant demonstrate consultation with residents of public housing in the preparation of the SAC application, and ensure ongoing consultation with the residents regarding the planning and administration of the health center?
Suggested Resource(s): SF-424A[ | Project Narrative[
[_] Yes [_ ] No [ X ] N/A
15. New or competing supplement applicant requesting PHPC and/or PHPC funding: Does the applicant attest on the Summary Page that funding will be utilized to supplement, and not supplant, the expenditures of the health center and the value of in-kind contributions for the delivery of services to these populations?
